metrics.label: Studies
metrics.value: Carnegie Mellon University · MSE Scalable Systems

about.heading: About me
about.body: I build native Android and Flutter cross-platform applications—from real-time collaborative image editors and expense-splitting apps to IoT privacy tools serving 100,000+ devices. My experience spans Android development at Adobe, research at Carnegie Mellon, and billing infrastructure at Amdocs handling SpaceX satellite events.

education.degree: Master of Software Engineering — Scalable Systems
B.Tech — Computer Science & Engineering and Business Systems

projects.name: CanvasX
projects.description: Real-time collaborative image editor with layers, blend modes, text tools, AI image generation, and Material 3 UI. GPU-assisted render pipeline using AGSL RuntimeShader and Firebase real-time collaboration with 50ms throttling.
projects.stack: Kotlin · Jetpack Compose · Firebase · AGSL · Room

projects.name: Dividr
projects.description: Native Android expense-splitting app with accurate equal-share expense logic and rounding adjustments for group reconciliation. Built with Clean Architecture, MVVM, Dagger Hilt, and SOLID principles.
projects.stack: Kotlin · Jetpack Compose · Firebase Auth · Firestore · Hilt

projects.name: HabitPro
projects.description: Flutter habit-building app that guides users through 21-day habit formation with category-based habits, custom habit creation, daily task progression, and AI-generated task suggestions.
projects.stack: Flutter · Dart · Appwrite Auth · Firebase Firestore · OpenAI API

projects.name: Newzzy
projects.description: Jetpack Compose news app that loads live trending news, supports keyword search, saves articles locally, and opens full article details in WebView.
projects.stack: Kotlin · Jetpack Compose · Clean Architecture · MVVM · Hilt · Retrofit · Room

bullets: Working under Digital Video and Ads team for the launch of an Android app of their flagship product "Premiere Pro."  Using state-of-the-art Android technologies around image and video editing.
Learning and adopting Jetpack Compose, Navigation3, Adaptive UI, Dependency Injection, Media3, and other modern libraries.
Following a highly structured engineering workflow with strict coding standards, automated checks, peer code reviews, controlled code pushes, and Agile sprint execution.
Researched under Prof. Norman Sadeh, CMU School of Computer Science on IoT privacy and security.

Developed a Flutter cross-platform application with primary focus on user security and privacy. Implemented the Data Rights Protocol (DRP) to help users discover and control data collected about them via IoT devices.
The application hosts over 100,000 IoT devices and users can discover the ones closest to them based on different device types like security cameras, audio sensors, etc.
Built automation tools in Python to reduce UNIX sandbox environment sanity time by 66% and manual PostgreSQL comparison from 2 days to 1 hour.
Worked under OPTUS team to rate events coming from SpaceX satellites via user SIM cards and enhanced SQL tables to support new data fields of SpaceX project.
Contributed to Amdocs billing architectural upgrade by updating Shell scripts and Perl jobs to a newer architecture, executed in scheduled Jenkins CI/CD pipelines and DAGs.
Leveraged PostgreSQL, Hive, Cloudera, Oracle DB, Jenkins and Kubernetes for the billing architectural upgrade.
Rolled out user gamification features in live Android applications having more than 1 million downloads on Play Store.
Led development of an internal application with Kotlin and MVVM architecture to manage more than 100 live ad campaigns spread across 5 applications on Play Store.
Volunteered to help and mentor college students at GreedyGame's Appicorns app dev hackathon.

items.title: A comprehensive review on NUI, multi-sensory interfaces and UX design for applications and devices for visually impaired users.
Comparative Analysis on Artificial Intelligence Technologies and its Application in FinTech.

contact.heading: Let’s connect
contact.body: Open for collaborations, full-time opportunities, or a chat about Android, Flutter, and systems engineering.
